title: knautia drymeja var. arvernensis
intro: Knautia drymeja var. arvernensis, also known as Alpine knawel, is a flowering plant native to Europe that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). Traditionally, Alpine knawel has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may provide health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. Alpine knawel can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and its dried flowers and leaves can be used in herbal remedies and supplements.
